question.text: What was the correct chronological order of derivatives product launches in India?

answer.text: Index futures (June 2000) -> Index options (June 2001) -> Stock options (July 2001) -> Stock futures (November 2001)
Stock futures (June 2000) -> Stock options (June 2001) -> Index options (July 2001) -> Index futures (November 2001)
Index options (June 2000) -> Index futures (June 2001) -> Stock futures (July 2001) -> Stock options (November 2001)
Index futures (June 2000) -> Stock futures (June 2001) -> Index options (July 2001) -> Stock options (November 2001)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The sequence of derivatives launches in India was: (1) Index futures — June 2000, (2) Index options — June 2001, (3) Stock options — July 2001, (4) Stock futures — November 2001. India started with index products before moving to individual stock products. **The Trap:** The other options shuffle the order. A common mistake is confusing whether stock options or stock futures came first (options preceded futures by about 4 months). **Cross-Exam Context:** This timeline is a high-priority fact for NISM Series VIII. Remember the mnemonic: "IF-IO-SO-SF" (Index Futures, Index Options, Stock Options, Stock Futures). **Key Takeaway:** India's derivative launch sequence: Jun 2000 (Index Futures) -> Jun 2001 (Index Options) -> Jul 2001 (Stock Options) -> Nov 2001 (Stock Futures).
